pr.title: Provenance-neutral terminology for the #157 architecture maps (drop evidence-free "legacy/old-foundation")
pr.description:
## Summary

Terminology-only correction to the maps merged in PR #157 (repo-local #47). The scan added there labeled earlier-introduced foundational modules as **"old-foundation" / "pre-#112 legacy" / "legacy pipeline."** Per the Program 7 operating rule, a component must not be called legacy/old/deprecated unless repo evidence proves it is unused, superseded, bypassed, or intentionally retired — none of these are. This PR replaces that wording with provenance-neutral terms and corrects one factual claim. **Mapping/wording only — no pipe/spine/bridge/orchestrator/runtime behavior changed.**

What changed:
- **Wording:** `old-foundation` / `pre-#112 legacy` / `legacy pipeline` / `older pipeline` → **"earlier-introduced foundational component"** across the tracked maps and the System Atlas data strings. `ExecutionTrace` vs `ExecutionReport` stays a **duplicate-candidate** (evidence-backed: both currently active) but is no longer called "legacy."
- **Section rename:** Duplicate/Alias Map "Old-foundation scan result" → **"Foundational provenance scan result"** (cross-reference in PR Purpose Map + behavior ledger updated to match).
- **Factual correction:** the PR Purpose Map previously claimed *"original creating-PR numbers are not repo-visible."* They are. This is a full (non-shallow) clone; `git log --diff-filter=A` recovers the introducing **GitHub repo-local** PR for each module:

  ```
  automation_bridge.py  -> repo-local PR #19
  pipe_client.py        -> repo-local PR #2   (earliest agents + named-pipe vertical slice)
  run_spine.py          -> repo-local PR #31
  automation_planner.py -> repo-local PR #35
  work_item_registry.py -> repo-local PR #56
  execution_attempt.py  -> repo-local PR #111
  schemas/orchestrator/mcp_layer/input_normalization/persistence -> initial-foundation commit (repo bootstrap, no PR)
  ```
  The map now cites these and notes GitHub repo-local PR numbers are distinct from Axiom **GPR** numbers, and that establishing them does **not** require the other Devin lane's provenance — this workspace's git history is sufficient. GPR-level provenance remains "not visible from this workspace" (marked as such, not invented).

## What did NOT change
- No component reclassified in substance (active/partial/duplicate-candidate statuses preserved; only the "legacy/old" label removed where unproven).
- No runtime code paths, schemas, atlas *structure*, or CLI behavior. The atlas still emits 25 families; only `name`/`status`/`notes`/`purpose` **strings** changed. Family list, aliases, and `primary_files` are untouched, so the atlas presence-checks and cross-references are unaffected.

## Files
- `docs/architecture/integration/PR_Purpose_Map_v0.md` — header, intro paragraph (provenance correction), one classification cell, cross-reference.
- `docs/architecture/integration/Duplicate_Alias_Map_v0.md` — Cluster 11 wording, scan-result section rename + wording, unknowns updated.
- `docs/logs/behavior-change-ledger.md` — BHV-031 wording synced to the renamed section/terms.
- `src/axiom_core/context_preflight.py` — System Atlas `name`/`status`/`notes`/`purpose` strings only.
- `tests/test_context_preflight.py` — test renamed to `test_atlas_includes_earlier_introduced_foundation_components`; assertion messages reworded (same assertions).

## Tests run
- `poetry run ruff check` on the two changed Python files — clean.
- `poetry run pytest tests/test_context_preflight.py -q` — **34 passed**. (Docs + atlas-string change; targeted test per tiered-testing policy.)

## Validation still pending / risks
- None functional. Risk is purely editorial (a missed occurrence of the old wording); grep confirms remaining "legacy" mentions are the intentional *"these are **not** legacy"* clarifications.

## Factory relevance
Strengthens the anti-duplication/context-enforcement layer (evidence quality of the maps): future architecture-sensitive PRs read accurate provenance and are not misled into treating active foundational components as retired. Does not touch discovery/primitive-validation/promotion runtime. 2024 baseline unaffected; no Revit-live validation required.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

Provenance-neutral terminology: replace 'legacy/old-foundation/pre-#112' wording in #157 maps

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
